info.name: Inherent API
info.description:
Postman collection for the Inherent stack — 23 requests across Health, Documents, Search, Chunks, and Ingestion (including dead-letter management).

Two services / two planes:
- **Public API** (`inh-public-api-svc`) on http://localhost:18000 — read + upload plane: documents, search, chunks. Auth: `X-API-Key` (must start with `ink_`) + `X-Workspace-Id`.
- **Ingestion API** on http://localhost:18002 — write/admin plane: it owns writes to PostgreSQL + Weaviate. Trigger/inspect ingestion, edit chunks, delete documents, read lineage, manage dead-letter jobs. Auth: its own `X-API-Key` (`INGESTION_API_KEY`, default `dev-ingestion-key`). No workspace header.

## Auth model
The collection sets `X-API-Key = {{api_key}}` at the collection level, so public requests inherit it. The **Ingestion** folder overrides `X-API-Key = {{ingest_key}}`. Public requests still carry an explicit `X-Workspace-Id` header (tenant routing, not auth).

## Setup
1. `docker compose up --build`
2. `make dev-seed` (creates API key `ink_dev_local_key_001` and workspace `ws_local_001`)
3. Import the companion environment `inherent.postman_environment.json` and select it (provides `api_base`, `ingest_base`, `api_key`, `workspace_id`, `ingest_key`, `user_id`, `document_id`).

## Editing the environment
To change a value (e.g. point at a remote host or paste a real `document_id`):
1. Left sidebar → **Environments** → click **Inherent Local**.
2. Edit the **Current Value** column (not Initial Value) for the row you want, then **Save** (Ctrl/Cmd+S).
3. Make sure **Inherent Local** is selected in the top-right environment dropdown so the change applies.

Gotcha: import populates **Initial Value**; requests and `pm.environment.set()` (e.g. the Upload script) read/write **Current Value**. Current Value stays local to your machine and is not included when you export/share the environment — so edits here will not show up in the committed JSON.

## Chaining
Run **Documents → Upload Document** first; its test script saves the returned `document_id` to the environment, so Get / Chunks / Search-filtered / Status / Edit / Delete / Lineage resolve automatically.

## Saved examples
Every request carries a saved example response, so you can see the expected shape in the Postman doc pane without running the stack. Examples use illustrative IDs.

## Smoke testing
Every request asserts its expected status code in Postman's test runner. `POST /ingest` is async — it returns **202 Accepted** by default (or `?wait=true` → **200** with the full result), and **409** if a workflow is already running; the tests accept those.

## Heads-up: Delete is destructive
**Ingestion → Delete Document** removes the document from PostgreSQL and its chunks from Weaviate. It is ordered after the read requests so it does not break the other chained requests.

item.name: Health
item.description: Liveness and readiness probes for both services. No auth required (the inherited X-API-Key header is ignored).

request.description: Liveness probe for the public API. No auth required (the inherited X-API-Key header is ignored).
Readiness probe. Checks PostgreSQL + Weaviate. Returns `degraded` (still 200) or 503 if a backing service is unhealthy.
Liveness probe for the ingestion service. Reports whether the Temporal worker is running. No auth.

item.name: Documents
item.description: Upload and read documents on the public API (port 18000). Auth: inherited X-API-Key (`ink_...`) + an explicit X-Workspace-Id header.

formdata.description: Pick a file. Allowed: text/plain, text/markdown, text/csv, text/html, application/pdf, application/json, application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document. Max 50 MB. e.g. docs/examples/sample-documents/sample.txt

request.description: Upload a document (multipart/form-data). Returns 201 with `document_id`, `status: pending`. The test script saves `document_id` to the environment.

response.name: 201 Created
response.body:
{
  "document_id": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",
  "name": "sample.txt",
  "workspace_id": "ws_local_001",
  "storage_url": "s3://inherent-documents/ws_local_001/3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6/sample.txt",
  "mime_type": "text/plain",
  "size_bytes": 842,
  "status": "pending",
  "message": "Document uploaded successfully. Processing will begin shortly."
}

request.description: List documents in the workspace with pagination.
Fetch a single document by ID. 404 if not found in the workspace.

item.name: Search
item.description: Semantic, hybrid, and keyword search over indexed chunks (public API, port 18000). Wait until a document shows `status: processed` before searching.

request.description: Default semantic (vector) search. Wait until documents are `processed` before searching.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "results": [
    {
      "chunk_id": "chunk-uuid-here",
      "document_id": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",
      "document_name": "sample.txt",
      "content": "Section 2: Key Concepts\nInherent is a backend system...",
      "score": 0.87,
      "metadata": { "chunk_index": 1, "token_count": 64 },
      "context_before": null,
      "context_after": null
    }
  ],
  "query": "what retrieval modes does Inherent support",
  "total_results": 1,
  "processing_time_ms": 42.3,
  "search_mode": "semantic",
  "total_tokens": 0
}

request.description: Hybrid search. `alpha` weights vector (1.0) vs keyword (0.0).

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "results": [
    {
      "chunk_id": "chunk-uuid-here",
      "document_id": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",
      "document_name": "sample.txt",
      "content": "Section 3: The ingestion pipeline parses, chunks, and embeds documents...",
      "score": 0.79,
      "metadata": { "chunk_index": 2, "token_count": 72 },
      "context_before": null,
      "context_after": null
    }
  ],
  "query": "document ingestion pipeline",
  "total_results": 1,
  "processing_time_ms": 51.6,
  "search_mode": "hybrid",
  "total_tokens": 0
}

request.description: Keyword (BM25) search.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{

request.description: Includes surrounding chunks per result (`context_before`/`context_after`).

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:

  "results": [

      "document_name": "sample.txt",
      "content": "Section 4: Supported file formats include txt, md, csv, html, pdf, json, docx.",

      "context_before": "Section 3: The ingestion pipeline parses, chunks, and embeds documents...",
      "context_after": "Section 5: Retrieval modes are semantic, hybrid, and keyword."
    }
  ],
  "query": "supported file formats",
  "total_results": 1,
  "processing_time_ms": 47.8,
  "search_mode": "semantic",
  "total_tokens": 0
}

request.description: Restrict search to specific document IDs.

response.body:

item.name: Chunks
item.description: Read a document's chunks, or the whole document combined into `full_text` for MCP-style context retrieval (public API, port 18000).

request.description: List all chunks for a document.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Returns document metadata + all chunks combined into `full_text`. For MCP-style full-document context retrieval.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "document": {
    "id": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",
    "name": "sample.txt",
    "workspace_id": "ws_local_001",
    "source_type": "upload",
    "mime_type": "text/plain",
    "size_bytes": 842,
    "chunk_count": 4,
    "status": "processed",
    "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:00:00+00:00",
    "updated_at": "2024-01-15T10:00:05+00:00",
    "metadata": null
  },
  "chunks": [
    {
      "id": "1",
      "document_id": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",
      "content": "Inherent Knowledge Base — Sample Text Document...",
      "chunk_index": 0,
      "token_count": 141,
      "metadata": null
    }
  ],
  "full_text": "Inherent Knowledge Base — Sample Text Document\n\n...\n\nSection 4: Retrieval Modes\n..."
}

item.name: Ingestion
item.description: Write/admin plane on the ingestion service (http://localhost:18002). All requests here authenticate with `X-API-Key = {{ingest_key}}` (set at this folder level) and do **not** take a workspace header. Covers triggering/inspecting ingestion, editing chunks, deleting documents, lineage, and dead-letter job management.

request.description: Manually (re-)ingest a file already in S3, bypassing the upload endpoint. Async by default: returns **202 Accepted** with a `workflow_id`. Returns **409** if a workflow is already running for this document, **503** if Temporal is unavailable.
Same as Trigger Ingestion but blocks until the workflow finishes and returns **200 OK** with the full result (`success`, `chunks_created`, `processing_time_ms`).

exec: pm.test('status is 200 or 404 (no running workflow)', () => pm.expect([200, 404]).to.include(pm.response.code));

request.description: Query the real-time progress of an ingestion workflow (`step`, `progress`, `chunks_created`). Returns 404 if no workflow exists or it is no longer queryable.

Edit chunk #0 of a document via a Temporal workflow (updates PostgreSQL + re-embeds in Weaviate). Replace the trailing `0` with the target chunk index. Returns 409 if an edit is already in progress, 500 if the edit fails.
Get data lineage for a document: an ordered list of ingestion pipeline step events (what happened during ingestion).

DESTRUCTIVE. Deletes the document from PostgreSQL and its chunks from Weaviate. Both `workspace_id` and `user_id` query params are required. Weaviate cleanup is best-effort (`weaviate_cleaned=false` if it fails). Ordered after the read requests so it does not break the other chained requests. Returns 404 if the document is not in PostgreSQL.

item.name: Dead-letter
item.description: Inspect and recover failed ingestion jobs that landed in the dead-letter table. List/get are read-only; retry re-publishes the original message; abandon marks a job permanently dead.

request.description: List dead-letter jobs. Optional filters: `workspace_id`, `status` (default `pending`), `limit` (1–200, default 50).
Get a single dead-letter job by numeric `job_id`. Returns 404 if not found. Replace `1` with a real job ID from the list.

exec: pm.test('status is 200, 404 (not found), 409 (bad status), or 500 (retry failed)', () => pm.expect([200, 404, 409, 500]).to.include(pm.response.code));

request.description: Retry a dead-letter job by re-publishing its original message. Only jobs with status `pending` or `retrying` can be retried (else 409). Returns 404 if the job is missing, 500 if re-trigger fails (status reset to pending).
Mark a dead-letter job as permanently abandoned. Returns 404 if the job is missing.
